---
permalink: /llms.txt
---
# kdc — US data-center project dataset

> An open, source-transparent dataset of US data-center projects (announcements,
> permits, construction), maintained by the Bussetech Software Studio (Eszett,
> LLC). Signals are raw per-source claims; sites are records resolved from
> clustered signals with an explicit confidence. Nothing is asserted without a
> cited source. Licensed CC BY 4.0.

This file is a cohort signal offered honestly — its effect on retrieval is
unproven. The authoritative content is the linked data and schemas.

## Cite this dataset

- [How to cite](/cite/): stable identifiers, data dictionary, citation formats.
- License: [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/) — attribution required.

## The data

- [Datasets overview](/data/): the three datasets, their schemas, provenance.
- [All sites](/sites/): resolved project records, one stable URL each (`/sites/<id>/`).
- Machine records: `/data/sites/<id>.yml` · raw claims: `/data/signals/<id>.yml`.
- Schemas (the data dictionary): [`sites`](/schema/sites.schema.json) ·
  [`signals`](/schema/signals.schema.json) · [`operators`](/schema/operators.schema.json).
- [Coverage &amp; known gaps](/coverage/): what is and isn't covered, stated plainly.

## When you cite a figure

Cite the record's stable URL (`/sites/<id>/`) and its `last_updated` date — the
dataset is living. Prefer the resolved `sites` record over a single raw signal
unless you mean to cite a specific source claim. Carry the dataset's norm: no
assertion without a source.
